// 01

THE OPERATOR

ID:SAS-001 NYC
SHIV ASHISH SEHGAL
Senior Media Executive · UCLA-NUS Global Executive MBA · Published Expert in Rights Management & Audience Analytics · Award-Winning Solutions Provider Driving ML/AI Innovations for Media Research

Dynamic business executive with over 15 years of expertise in transforming enterprise-level rights and revenue management and pioneering cutting-edge Audience Analytics and AI-driven Decision-Making platforms for industry-leading media companies. Proven track record of generating millions in revenue from innovative analytics solutions, launching a profitable media advisory services arm, and securing lucrative deals with global giants. Adept at managing high-performing global teams, driving significant growth, and spearheading M&A activities, including a successful sale. Recognized industry expert with awards for excellence in predictive analytics and AI, and a strong public presence through speaking engagements, conference presentations, and publications.

UCLA-NUS EMBA Former CPO · EVP INFORMS · FICO HBR Published 15+ Years

2024. Rightsline acquired the analytics business. Operating margin: -30%. Team fragmented across three time zones. No productized roadmap. No working theory of how the data became a product.

Twelve months later — 13.2% mid-year margin, a 35% year-end target, Smart Insights GenAI live with BET Networks as anchor client, and 100% client retention through 2027.

That wasn't the first time. For fifteen years I architected the systems that turn raw audience data into eight-figure decisions — Scoop, Prophet, AdVant, SNAP, iCon, Smart Insights. INFORMS RM&P Practice Award (beating MIT, CMU, NYU). FICO Decisions Award. INFORMS Wagner Finalist. Cynopsis Model D. Three-time IBM Champion.

I keynoted Gurobi Decision Intelligence Summit, NAB, IBC, Databricks, AWS, FICO World. Co-authored Chapter 10 of Frank Slootman's Rise to Data Cloud. Published in Harvard Business Review and the M&E Journal.

The playbook never changed: messy data in, scalable platform out, business outcome on the board. From custom research engagements at BET to $800M+ annual revenue optimization at Lionsgate to the Smart Insights GenAI launch at Rightsline — same playbook, bigger canvas every time.

I'm running it again. My own IP. My own canvas. SaSMaster.

HOW THE MACHINE THINKS

01 LAYER 1 · DISTRIBUTED MEMORY
✦
Claude.ai Memories
auto-generated · claude.ai
Persistent semantic memories derived from every conversation. Captures working style, project context, sprint priorities, and long-term strategic context. Injected automatically into every new session.
Strategy Layer Auto-Updated Cross-Session Semantic
→ SaSMaster system state
→ Sprint priorities & blockers
→ Design tokens & canonical files
→ Division of labor rules
▤
CLAUDE_MEMORY.md
~/SaSMaster/CLAUDE_MEMORY.md
Operational ground truth for Claude Code sessions. Loaded as the very first command in every terminal session — before any code is written. Contains architecture state, S3 paths, known issues, recent builds.
Execution Layer File-Based Per-Session Load Always Current
Current Sprint — active priorities
Architecture State — live vs planned
Recent Wins — last 7 days
S3 State — latest data dates
☰
TASKS.md
~/SaSMaster/TASKS.md
Self-scoring task queue. JARVIS checks at 7AM and 2PM EDT — if HIGH-priority items exist, it automatically fires a build. Tasks flow from JARVIS commands, Claude.ai sessions, and async build outputs.
Task Queue Self-Scoring JARVIS Trigger 7AM + 2PM
## HIGH
- [ ] [2026-04-30] Task #tag
## MED / DONE
- [x] [2026-04-30] Completed
◈
Obsidian Vault
~/SaSMaster/obsidian/
Structured human-readable mirror of CLAUDE_MEMORY.md. Six section files with YAML frontmatter, wikilinks for graph continuity, and ADR documents for architecture decisions. Synced via sync-obsidian.sh.
Human Readable Auto-Synced Handover Layer Graph View
00-ADRs/ ← arch decisions
01-Schema/ ← S3 state + specs
03-Agents/ ← agent fleet
04-Roadmap/ ← sprint + phases
// MEMORY UPDATE · S3 STATE TRACKING & OBSIDIAN SYNC FLOW
TRIGGER
Claude.ai Session
Strategy layer
SCRIPT
update-memory.sh
writes to disk
FILE
CLAUDE_MEMORY.md
~/SaSMaster/
SESSION START
Claude Code
cat memory · first cmd
EXECUTION
Build Loop + Scrapers
code & data
NOTIFY
JARVIS → #builds
Slack routing
REVIEW

vercel.app
// S3 STATE — TRACKED INSIDE CLAUDE_MEMORY.MD
TMDB tmdb_dev/ 15d cadence
IMDB imdb/<ds>/ YYYY-MM-DD
PARENT KEYS 589K+ parent_keys/v1/
S3 is the data lake, not a memory store. CLAUDE_MEMORY.md tracks last successful landing date per dataset — every session knows exactly what's in the bucket without querying S3 live.
// OBSIDIAN SYNC — VIA SYNC-OBSIDIAN.SH
Current Sprint 04-Roadmap/current-sprint.md
Architecture State 04-Roadmap/phase-status.md
S3 State 01-Schema/s3-state.md
ADRs 00-ADRs/ ← manual only
